behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at watcher-to-OS event boundary (recursive watch coverage), not just local parsing logic. REAL-INFRA: depends on real filesystem event delivery for nested paths. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher events can falsely prove recursive behavior even if real watcher subscription misses subdirectories. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss a misconfigured recursive watch option because path-handling helpers still pass. Integration (with fake/in-process event emitter) would miss OS-specific non-delivery of subdirectory create/write events.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp directory tree. Create `root/sub/a.txt` after startup and assert exactly one transformed output is written for that nested file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is primarily module handoff/state (event intake -> dedup store -> scheduler), not OS primitives. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess/OS dependency required to expose core failure. MOCK-HIDE: pure mocks are enough if they preserve module boundaries; full unit of dedup function alone may miss config/wiring mistakes. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text focuses on dedup time-window semantics, not real FS event quirks.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real daemon modules in-process with a controllable clock. Submit two processing requests for identical path inside the configured window and assert only one pipeline execution; then advance clock past window and assert next request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at real OS event burst/coalescing behavior crossing watcher->deduper/queue boundaries. REAL-INFRA: explicitly depends on operating system emitting multiple events. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single/clean events can pass while real OS emits create+change+rename sequences causing duplicates. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses inter-module race between event ingestion and dedup commit. Integration with fake events misses true event timing/order patterns from kernel/watch backend.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher on disk. Perform one write pattern known to emit multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename or append sequence) and assert exactly one output artifact and one pipeline invocation for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears in watcher lifecycle against real inode/path invalidation and re-subscription. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem delete/recreate semantics while process is alive. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher can emit idealized reattach events and hide backend-specific failure to re-register. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses long-lived watcher state transitions entirely. Integration with fake FS events misses real handle invalidation after directory replacement.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real directory, delete watched directory, recreate same path, then create a new file in recreated directory. Assert daemon processes it without restart and without manual reconfiguration.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering depends on interaction between filesystem timestamp metadata, event arrival race, and scheduler. REAL-INFRA: creation timestamp granularity/availability is OS/filesystem dependent. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps/events can enforce clean ordering and hide ties or coarse-resolution timestamp bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses mismatch between stat metadata and queue insertion timing. Integration with fake metadata misses real ctime/birthtime resolution collisions that break intended ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files nearly simultaneously on real filesystem with controlled timestamp differences (or known ties), then assert processing sequence matches specified creation-time ordering policy (including tie-break behavior if defined).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is timing-sensitive across real writer process, filesystem flush behavior, and watcher events. REAL-INFRA: needs real partial-write conditions and OS event timing. MOCK-HIDE: fake completed writes hide premature pickup of incomplete content. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses race between file readiness check and ongoing write stream. Integration with fake FS cannot reproduce true write-in-progress visibility/flush timing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real writer that streams file content in chunks with delays. Trigger arrival event on first chunk, verify daemon does not process until writer closes/flushed, then assert output reflects full final content only once.
